title: elatine macropoda f. hungarica
intro: Elatine macropoda f. hungarica, also known as the Hungarian waterwort, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Elatinaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, inflammation, and skin conditions. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been found to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, contributing to its health benefits such as improving immune function and protecting against oxidative stress. Elatine macropoda f. hungarica can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in some traditional folk medicine practices in Eastern Europe.
